stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
An aeroplane, whose specific data is shown in the annex, has a planned Take-off Mass of 200 000 kg, with its Centre of Gravity (CG) located 15.38 m rearward of the reference point, representing a CG location at 30% MAC (Mean Aerodynamic Chord). The current cargo load distribution is:

Front cargo: 6 500 kg
Rear cargo: 4 000 kg
For performance purposes, the captain decides to reset the value of the centre of gravity location to 33% MAC. The front and rear cargo compartments are located at a distance of 15 m and 25 m from the reference point respectively. After the transfer operation, the new cargo load distribution is:

options.a: front cargo: 6 760 kg; rear cargo: 3 740 kg.
options.b: front cargo: 4 550 kg; rear cargo: 5 950 kg.
options.c: front cargo: 9 260 kg; rear cargo: 1 240 kg.
options.d: front cargo: 3 740 kg; rear cargo: 6 760 kg.
